column8: I was surprised that both and Mary and Oliver were late for the meeting.
column9: My mum said she was both of proud and nervous when I appeared in the school play.
column10: Why don't both of you wait here while I go and see if Stuart is in?
column11: Both of books have got pages missing.
column12: Can I throw and both these magazines away, Derek?
column13: When I won the race, I was and exhausted and happy.
column14: Edward and Nigel both of them wanted to go on holiday, but they couldn't afford it.
column15: Did both you and Sylvia grow up in the south of France?
